example: I am a journalist who reports news from around the world.
ja: ジャーナリスト、報道記者

example: My job is to talk to people from all walks of life.
ja: 人生のあらゆる分野、社会的背景

example: I spent the entire summer at a summer camp in the United States.
I decided to put on a brave face and embrace everything about the American way of life.
ja: 受け入れる、抱きしめる

example: They were trying to make a correlation with the world they knew.
When you're different, you have to work at the longing.
My assigned roommate was eagerly waiting for me.
ja: 割り当てられたルームメイト

example: When we label someone as different, it dehumanizes them.
ja: ラベルを貼る、分類する

example: Labeling someone different can dehumanize them.
How do we recognize our blind spots?
I participated in a talent auction for charity.
I got up on stage with my little boombox and pressed play.
ja: ブームボックス、ポータブルラジオ

example: At 24 years old, I was becoming a refugee of sorts.
I came to post-racial America, where the notion of us and them is being eroded.
zh: 後種族的，種族隔閡消失的
ja: ポスト人種的（人種差別がなくなった状態）

example: A large part of the electorate sees them as the others.
Minority groups often face hatred and intolerance.
Censorship was growing in my country.
I watched election results with undocumented families.
She asked me if her mom was going to be deported.
I hit the worst traumatic bump in the road yet.
My sister spent most of the time in a wheelchair.
She had a sharp wit and a kind heart.
You can't let those differences define you; you have to reimagine yourself.
People with physical or neurological difficulties face challenges.
Environmentally impacted communities often face hardships.
ja: 環境に影響を受けたコミュニティ

example: Women who've been sexually assaulted need support.
Athletes bend their knee as a sign of protest.
Sometimes society tells us we don't fit the mold.
We are all quirky and unique.
Let's be humanists before and after everything else.
